meta.description: Why a person started boxing. Rolled once at generation; weights sum to 1.0. This is surface-level information — it is part of what the player learns early about a fighter (ocean rule: the visible layer). The reason for boxing will eventually influence soul trait reveal patterns — for example, a fighter who boxes as an outlet may reveal emotional traits sooner under pressure. That link is not implemented here; it is noted for the moment system to wire up once soul reveals are built.

reasons.label: Needed an outlet
reasons.description: Had too much anger, energy, or pain and needed somewhere to put it. Boxing absorbed it. Still does.

reasons.label: Had something to prove
reasons.description: Someone doubted them, dismissed them, or made them feel small. Boxing is the answer they're still writing.

reasons.label: Saw it as a way out
reasons.description: The neighbourhood, the job, the future that was already written. Boxing was the door that wasn't supposed to be there.

reasons.label: Pure passion for the sport
reasons.description: Loved it from the first time they saw it. No trauma, no angle — just the sport itself.

reasons.label: Fell into it
reasons.description: Didn't plan it. A gym was nearby, a friend dragged them along, someone said they had natural hands. One thing led to another.

reasons.label: Family tradition
reasons.description: Father boxed. Or an uncle. Or a grandfather. The gym was just where the family went.

reasons.label: A friend brought me
reasons.description: The simplest origin. A friend was going and brought them along. The friend may have quit. They didn't.
